commit.message:
fix: remove hand-time +0.24s adjustment from sort_value computation

The standardizer already applies the adjustment when populating
standardized_result. Adding it again in convert_result_to_seconds
caused display/sort mismatch for results where standardized_result
was NULL (relay splits sorted slower than their displayed time).

commit.message:
Fix CI: remove recursive submodule init, update content submodule

The content repo had orphaned gitlink entries (submodule references
without URLs) that caused --recursive submodule init to fail. Fixed
in two ways:
- Content repo: removed the 6 orphaned gitlink entries
- CI workflow: changed to init only the content submodule explicitly

Also includes plan 0051 (git-commit-specification chain).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(hooks): éliminer les hook errors systématiques sur chaque Bash

Cause : set -euo pipefail dans guard_dangerous_cmds.sh et post_edit_lint.sh
+ INPUT="$(cat)" sans fallback → si stdin reçoit SIGPIPE ou EOF inattendu,
cat exit 1, set -e tue le script → Claude Code affiche "hook error" sur
TOUTES les commandes Bash.

- guard_dangerous_cmds.sh : set -euo → set -uo + INPUT cat || true
  (exit 2 intentionnel pour blocage préservé)
- post_bash_pipeline.sh : INPUT cat → cat || true (set -uo déjà OK)
- post_edit_lint.sh : set -euo → set -uo + INPUT cat || true
- settings.json : onError:"continue" sur tous les hooks non-bloquants
  (PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart, Stop, etc.)
  PreToolUse guard_dangerous_cmds garde son comportement bloquant (exit 2)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: tenant management redesign — invite flows, onboarding, detail drawer, team management

Phase 15: Complete tenant management overhaul with email-based invite system,
admin onboarding, rich tenant detail drawer, and tenant-scoped team management.

- Email-based admin invite via Supabase inviteUserByEmail on tenant creation
- /onboarding/confirm-tenant page for invited admins to confirm settings + set password
- /onboarding/set-password page for invited agents/assistants
- Middleware redirect for users with onboarding_completed: false
- Tenant detail slide-over drawer with 8 cards (header, seats, health score,
  usage sparklines, top personas, growth trend, quota, tabbed activity)
- 3-level drill-down: table → drawer → full activity log modal
- /[orgId]/team page for tenant admins to manage and invite team members
- 5 new API endpoints under /api/admin/tenants/[id]/
- 6 new activity log action types for tenant lifecycle events
- Role-gated Team nav item in sidebar

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(graph): add RGCN structural embedding pipeline with CLI integration

Implements the GraphSAGE/RGCN inductive embedding pipeline as HADES core
infrastructure. Two-layer RGCN with basis decomposition trained via link
prediction on the NL knowledge graph (1390 nodes, 3838 edges, 21 relation
types). Produces 128-dim structural embeddings stored alongside Jina V4
content embeddings as the fourth compliance signal.

New module core/graph/:
- loader.py: ArangoDB → PyG data pipeline with bidirectional ID mapping
- model.py: RGCNEncoder with learned type embeddings for unembedded nodes
- train.py: Training loop with early stopping and ArangoDB export

New CLI commands (hades graph-embed):
- train: Train RGCN on any database, export embeddings back
- embed: Retrieve structural embedding for a specific node
- neighbors: Find k-nearest structural neighbors across collections

Training results on NL_graph_v0: 80.3% test accuracy, 0.870 AUC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix tooltip clipping near top of viewport

Use explicit tooltipHeight (160px) to determine above/below placement
instead of an arbitrary threshold. Above case now uses top (rect.top -
gap - tooltipHeight) instead of bottom-based positioning, which was
miscalculating and clipping off-screen. Simplified to always use
position:fixed with top/left — no more bottom property.

commit.message:
feat: complete PostgreSQL compatibility for all functional tests (#46)

* feat: complete PostgreSQL compatibility for all functional tests

- IdType: platform-aware getSQLDeclaration (BYTEA for PostgreSQL, BINARY(16)
  for MySQL) and getBindingType override returning ParameterType::BINARY
- CI: expand PostgreSQL job to run full --testsuite Functional (was filtered
  to OrderedOutboxTransportTest only)
- Tests: update docstrings to reflect database-portable test scope

All 148 tests pass. All 23 functional tests pass on both MySQL and PostgreSQL.

Refs #43, #39, #38

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address code review findings for PostgreSQL compatibility

- IdType: delegate getSQLDeclaration to platform->getBinaryTypeDeclarationSQL()
  instead of manual instanceof check — leverages DBAL's own platform abstraction
- IdType: remove getBindingType() override — call sites already specify
  ParameterType::BINARY explicitly, and the override broke DBAL v3 compat
  (ParameterType is int in v3, enum in v4)
- CI: remove duplicate functional-tests-postgres job — the matrix-based
  functional-tests job already covers both MySQL and PostgreSQL
- Tests: use real MySQLPlatform/PostgreSQLPlatform instances instead of stubs
  for SQL declaration tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix incomplete candle bug in CheckExit RSI calculation

CheckExit was using the incomplete (in-progress) last candle for RSI
calculation, causing premature exits. Evidence: SOL short had RSI drop
75→29.6 in 1 hour with only $0.03 price change — the incomplete candle
distorted RSI. Now uses completedCandles for RSI while keeping the
latest candle for real-time price.

Affects both Upbit scalp (crypto_scalp.go) and Binance short scalp
(short_scalp.go). The Scan (entry) side was already fixed on 03-08,
but CheckExit (exit) was missed.

commit.message:
fix: prevent MCP content double-wrapping for pre-formatted handler responses

Handlers like ManageConversationHandler and GetAnalyticsHandler return
properly formatted MCP content arrays ({content: [{type: 'text', text: ...}]}).
The server.js normalizer was extracting result.content (the array) and
re-wrapping it as text, causing MCP SDK validation failures. Now detects
pre-formatted responses and passes them through directly.

commit.message:
fix(auth): bypass navigator.locks in createBrowserClient — fix LockManager timeout

The WebLocks API (navigator.locks) is used by supabase-js v2 to serialize
token refresh operations across browser tabs competing for localStorage.

With @supabase/ssr (cookie-based storage), the server middleware already
serializes token operations — each request goes through middleware which
reads/writes cookies atomically. The WebLock is therefore redundant.

Problem: the new multi-path init (getSession + INITIAL_SESSION + safety
timeout) launches concurrent auth operations. All of them try to acquire
the same exclusive lock "lock:sb-hstlbkudwnboebmarilp-auth-token". One
holds it while running; when the user attempts login, signInWithPassword
also needs the lock, waits 10 seconds, then times out.

Fix: override the lock function to execute immediately without acquiring
any lock. This is the canonical solution recommended by Supabase for SSR
apps using @supabase/ssr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
